name: legal-compliance
title: Legal & Compliance
description: Adapts the harness to produce legal and compliance deliverables — contracts, clause sets, compliance memos — where the failure mode is an unenforceable obligation, a defined term that no clause uses, or a citation to authority that does not resolve, not a failing build.

stages.label: Frame
stages.purpose: Define the matter, the parties, the objective, and the governing jurisdiction (and venue) the instrument will operate under.
stages.output: Matter note: parties, objective, governing jurisdiction

stages.label: Structure
stages.purpose: Lay out the clause architecture, allocate risk between the parties, and fix the defined terms before any operative language is drafted. The plan the draft must follow.

stages.label: Draft
stages.purpose: Write the instrument: clauses, defined terms, obligations — each obligation clear and bounded, each authority traceable to the brief.

stages.label: Review breadth
stages.purpose: Scan every clause for completeness and internal consistency; flag missing sections, dead definitions, unbounded obligations.

stages.label: Review depth
stages.purpose: Run the gate verdicts: jurisdiction alignment, defined-term consistency, enforceability, obligation completeness, authority traceability.

stages.label: Feedback
stages.purpose: Evaluate reviewer challenges, reconcile the risk allocation, finalize the execution-ready instrument.

gates.name: Jurisdiction Alignment
gates.question: Is the governing law and venue stated explicitly, and is every clause consistent with that jurisdiction (no conflicting forum or choice-of-law language)?

gates.name: Defined-Term Consistency
gates.question: Is every defined term actually used in the operative text, and is there no dead definition left over from a template?

gates.name: Enforceability
gates.question: Is each obligation clear, bounded, and lawful — no vague, open-ended, or unenforceable duty?

gates.name: Obligation Completeness
gates.question: Are each party's duties, the remedies for breach, and the termination conditions all covered?

gates.name: Authority Traceability
gates.question: Does every citation to a statute, regulation, or precedent resolve to a stated authority — none invented or dangling?
